profile.program: Master of Information and Data Science (MIDS)

report.cost_of_living: Berkeley's cost of living is notably high, with average monthly rents for one-bedroom apartments around $2,500. Utilities and groceries also tend to be above the national average, impacting overall expenses.
report.academic: The MIDS program offers a comprehensive curriculum covering machine learning, data engineering, and AI ethics. It emphasizes practical applications, preparing students for real-world data science challenges.
report.visa: International students must secure an F-1 visa, which requires proof of sufficient financial resources and enrollment in a full-time program. The MIDS program's online format may have specific visa implications; consult the university's international office for detailed guidance.
report.culture_shock: Berkeley's diverse and progressive environment can be both exciting and overwhelming. Adapting to local customs, academic rigor, and social dynamics may require time and openness.
report.community: The MIDS program fosters a collaborative online community, offering virtual networking events and discussion forums. However, the online nature may limit in-person interactions compared to on-campus programs.
report.career: Graduates have pursued roles in top tech companies, leveraging the program's strong industry connections and career services. The curriculum's focus on practical skills aligns well with current market demands.
report.safety: Berkeley has areas with varying safety levels. While the campus and surrounding neighborhoods are generally safe, it's advisable to stay informed about local safety guidelines and resources.
report.climate: Berkeley enjoys a Mediterranean climate with mild, wet winters and dry summers. Average temperatures range from 50°F in winter to 70°F in summer, providing a comfortable environment year-round.
report.part_time_work: International students on an F-1 visa are typically allowed to work up to 20 hours per week on-campus during academic terms. Off-campus employment requires specific authorization, such as Curricular Practical Training (CPT).

health: Managing screen time and ergonomics due to the online nature of the program.
Potential stress from balancing coursework with personal responsibilities.
Limited physical activity opportunities compared to on-campus programs.
mood: Feelings of isolation due to the online format; proactive engagement in virtual communities is essential.
Adapting to self-paced learning and maintaining motivation without in-person interactions.
Potential challenges in building a support network remotely.
money: High tuition costs associated with the MIDS program.
Limited opportunities for on-campus employment due to the online format.
Potential savings on commuting and relocation expenses.
city_major_specific_challenges: Navigating time zone differences for live sessions if residing outside Berkeley.
Accessing local resources and libraries may be limited for remote students.
Building professional networks without physical presence in tech hubs.
Managing asynchronous coursework alongside live sessions.
Staying updated with rapidly evolving data science tools and methodologies.
Ensuring reliable internet connectivity for uninterrupted participation.
Balancing program demands with personal and professional commitments.
Engaging in practical projects without direct access to campus facilities.

program_profile.official_name: Master of Information and Data Science
program_profile.visa_eligible_notes: International students should consult the university's international office regarding visa eligibility for online programs.
milestones: Completion of 27 units of coursework over three to five terms.
Participation in at least one 2-3 day immersion on the UC Berkeley campus.
Completion of a capstone project integrating core concepts learned throughout the program.
prerequisites: Proficiency in programming languages such as Python.
Background in statistics and linear algebra.
Relevant professional experience in data science or related fields is recommended.
admissions: Online application through the UC Berkeley Graduate Division.
Statement of purpose and personal history statement.
Official transcripts from all post-secondary institutions attended.
deadlines: Application deadlines vary; refer to the program's official website for the most current information.
funding: Limited scholarships and financial aid options are available; applicants are encouraged to explore external funding sources.

student_life_profile.housing: As an online program, students are not required to relocate to Berkeley, potentially reducing housing expenses.
student_life_profile.commute: No commuting is necessary due to the online format, allowing for flexible study schedules.
student_life_profile.campus_support: Access to virtual academic advising, technical support, and career services.
student_life_profile.community: Opportunities to engage with peers through online forums, group projects, and virtual events.
student_life_profile.safety: Safety considerations are primarily related to online security and data privacy.
student_life_profile.climate: Not applicable due to the online nature of the program.

career_profile.local_industry: The San Francisco Bay Area is a global tech hub, offering numerous opportunities in data science and related fields.
career_profile.internship: While the program is online, students are encouraged to seek internships to gain practical experience; support is available through career services.
career_profile.work_authorization: International students should verify work authorization requirements and opportunities with the university's international office.
career_profile.language_or_networking_requirements: Proficiency in English is required; networking opportunities are facilitated through virtual events and the program's online community.
alumni_outcomes: Data Scientist at leading tech companies

notable_courses.title: Applied Data Engineering
notable_courses.note: Focuses on designing, building, and maintaining data pipelines.

notable_courses.title: Applied Machine Learning
notable_courses.note: Covers end-to-end model development and deployment.

notable_courses.title: Modern Artificial Intelligence (AI) Strategy and Applications
notable_courses.note: Explores data management, machine learning strategy, and product innovation.
